## Q1 quote wraps a line in the source
What does entropy measure?

- [x] The average surprise of a random variable.
- [ ] The number of distinct symbols in the alphabet.
- [ ] The length of the shortest codeword.
- [ ] The redundancy of a fixed-length code.

quote: average surprise of a random variable, in bits.
location: Notes/sample.md

## Q2 quote is present verbatim
What is the channel capacity?

- [x] The maximum mutual information over input distributions.
- [ ] The mutual information for the uniform input distribution.
- [ ] The entropy of the output alphabet.
- [ ] The minimum achievable code rate.

quote: maximum mutual information over input distributions
location: Notes/sample.md

## Q3 quote is not in the source
What bounds does entropy satisfy?

- [x] It is non-negative and at most the log of the alphabet size.
- [ ] It equals the variance of the distribution.
- [ ] It grows linearly with the number of samples.
- [ ] It is the reciprocal of the mutual information.

quote: entropy is bounded below by the free energy of the ensemble
location: Notes/sample.md
